blockers: The fallback accepts an archive after any one non-metadata member verifies, while explicitly continuing past other unattested regular files; an archive containing an attested binary plus an unattested executable or configuration file is therefore accepted without complete subject coverage.
Archive extraction and traversal failures are converted to NoAttestations, so the security-sensitive fallback can downgrade into later provenance or no-provenance handling instead of failing closed.
The implementation does not connect the GitHub backend to archive-aware multi-subject SLSA verification, and the deterministic semantic verifier rejects it.
overall_reasoning: The agent performed broad investigation of the GitHub backend, sigstore bridge, extraction helpers, provenance settings, and adjacent tests, then preserved archive-blob GitHub attestation as the first attempt. However, it committed to a different trust model than the task requires: it extracts the archive in the backend, queries GitHub artifact attestations separately for each member, skips a hand-built class of metadata, and accepts as soon as verified_count is nonzero. Unattested non-metadata regular files are explicitly ignored, so the accepted archive is not fully covered. Extraction and file-walk failures are also mapped to NoAttestations, contradicting fail-closed behavior. The deterministic verifier's alternative-path check independently rejects the backend because it does not call an archive-aware verifier; the subsequent hidden-test compile diagnostics reflect the missing multi-subject SLSA integration, but the outcome is valid without grading those implementation names because the patch's own control flow exposes the subject-coverage and downgrade defects. Testing effort was substantial at the repository level (938 unit tests plus focused GitHub tests and cargo check), but the new tests cover only metadata classification and recursive collection. The added executable e2e file only prints success and does not exercise verification, and there is no behavioral test for mixed attested/unattested members or extraction failure. The agent did not use the available clarification channel despite explicitly recognizing consequential ambiguity in the trust boundary. This security-sensitive patch is not mergeable.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The helper names and comments make the intended flow readable, but the large policy comment surface, hand-maintained metadata classification, leaked kept temporary directory, and no-op e2e script add complexity without establishing correctness.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The patch uses existing archive and GitHub sigstore APIs and stays in the GitHub backend, but implements repeated per-member network verification at the application layer instead of using the repository's archive-aware multi-subject SLSA verifier path.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Changes are limited to the GitHub backend and one e2e file, but the 200-plus-line backend addition and a test that only echoes success are disproportionate and the implementation follows the wrong provenance path.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Archive-level verification remains first and non-NoAttestations cryptographic errors propagate, but accepting any positive member while ignoring other unattested regular files and downgrading extraction/traversal failures are fundamental fail-open defects.
rating_rationales.test_quality: The agent ran focused GitHub tests, cargo check, formatting, and all 938 mise unit tests. New tests exercise only file collection and metadata-name heuristics; they do not test the verification policy, mixed coverage, failure propagation, archive safety, or the claimed e2e behavior.

strengths: Investigated the existing GitHub attestation, SLSA, lockfile, download, and extraction flows before editing.
Preserved archive-blob GitHub attestation as the first verification attempt and propagated explicit cryptographic verification errors.
Added focused helper tests and ran the full mise unit suite, cargo check, and formatting.
summary: Thorough investigation and broad local verification, but incomplete subject coverage and downgrade-on-extraction-error violate the required fail-closed archive trust boundary.
